raw_consumer_statement:
XX/XX/XXXX I used Barclay credit card in the amount of $ {$6700.00} to make payment to XXXX XXXX for a trip to take place XXXX XXXX XXXX. I paid back the entire amount to Barclay before the end of XXXX. In XXXX I also paid XXXX XXXX with my credit card in the amount of {$750.00} and {$300.00} paid to XXXX. Early XXXX I called to request ticket information and could not reach the company. What was odd, I think XXXX XXXX used XXXX travel agency for the {$300.00}. Early XXXX I attempted continuous phone calls to reach XXXX  XXXX  with no response. I contacted Barclay and told them I was fraudulently taken for. They replied they will look into the matter and refunded the the total amount professionally until they investigated. I used the cash refunded and worked with XXXX because, XXXX XXXX was really crafty by putting a {$500.00} down for a XXXX XXXX XXXX  so the air flights were reserved but not paid. In order to keep the reservation because we were attending a wedding and cold not make a change in date, I had to use their vendors for the hotel in XXXX and I had to pay the hotel in XXXX, they only made a reservation for. When I returned from the trip I called Barclay. They sent a letter of denied for the claim of Fraud. Then I disputed the denial and provided documentation Barclay replied they would investigate. In the meantime the interest rate is increasing for the full payment amount every day. Barclay was able to recover the {$750.00} from International Travel and {$300.00} from XXXX during the dispute period. I was informed on XX/XX/XXXX they are unable to dispute in my favor due to the following : " We are outside of the permitted timeframes to continue the dispute process. Insufficient proof that the merchant violated the invoice/contract 's terms and conditions. Any further to dispute this transaction must be made directly with the merchant. '' There is no merchant. 
How is this not fraud? By their actions there is definitely a connection between XXXX XXXX and XXXX. Is that Fraud? Thank you for your consideration.
